title: Setup Python Environment and Dependencies
description: Install PyTorch, torchvision, and required packages for contrastive learning experiments

steps.step2: Install PyTorch 2.0+ with CUDA 11.8: pip install torch torchvision torchaudio --index-url https://download.pytorch.org/whl/cu118
steps.step3: Install additional dependencies: pip install numpy scipy scikit-learn matplotlib seaborn tqdm tensorboard
steps.step4: Verify GPU availability: python -c 'import torch; print(torch.cuda.is_available(), torch.cuda.get_device_name(0))'
steps.step5: Create directory structure: mkdir -p data checkpoints results logs/figures

title: Download and Prepare CIFAR-100 Dataset
description: Download CIFAR-100 with both coarse (20 superclasses) and fine (100 classes) labels for feature suppression evaluation

steps.step1: CIFAR-100 will be automatically downloaded via torchvision.datasets.CIFAR100(root='./data', download=True)
steps.step2: Verify dataset structure: 50,000 training images, 10,000 test images, 100 fine classes, 20 coarse superclasses
steps.step3: Define data augmentation: RandomResizedCrop(32, scale=(0.2, 1.0)), RandomHorizontalFlip(), ColorJitter(0.4, 0.4, 0.4, 0.1), RandomGrayscale(p=0.2), Normalize((0.5071, 0.4867, 0.4408), (0.2675, 0.2565, 0.2761))
steps.step4: Create two-view augmentation pipeline for contrastive learning using torchvision.transforms
steps.step5: Create dataloader with batch_size=256, num_workers=4, pin_memory=True

title: Baseline 1: Cross-Entropy Training on CIFAR-100
description: Train ResNet-18 with standard cross-entropy loss as simple baseline. This is the most basic supervised learning approach.

steps.step1: Implement standard ResNet-18 architecture (from torchvision.models.resnet18, modify first conv for CIFAR: kernel_size=3, stride=1, padding=1; remove maxpool)
steps.step3: Optimizer: SGD with momentum=0.9, weight_decay=5e-4, initial_lr=0.1
steps.step4: Learning rate schedule: Cosine annealing from 0.1 to 0.0 over 200 epochs
steps.step6: Save final model checkpoints and training logs to checkpoints/ce_cifar100_seed{N}.pth
steps.step7: Record final test accuracy for each seed

title: Baseline 2: Supervised Contrastive Learning (SCL) on CIFAR-100
description: Implement standard Supervised Contrastive Learning (Khosla et al. 2020) as strong baseline. This is the state-of-the-art method we aim to improve.

steps.step1: Implement SCL loss: L = -1/|P| * sum_{p in P} log(exp(z_i·z_p/τ) / sum_{a in A} exp(z_i·z_a/τ))
steps.step2: Architecture: ResNet-18 backbone + 2-layer MLP projector (dim=128) for contrastive learning
steps.step4: Train encoder with SCL loss for 200 epochs, then train linear classifier for 100 epochs on frozen features
steps.step5: Optimizer: SGD with momentum=0.9, weight_decay=1e-4, lr=0.5 for encoder (scaled by batch_size/256), lr=1.0 for classifier
steps.step6: Learning rate: Cosine annealing, warmup for first 10 epochs
steps.step9: Record encoder training time for computational overhead comparison

title: FD-SCL: Feature-Diversity-Aware Supervised Contrastive Learning on CIFAR-100
description: Train our proposed FD-SCL method on CIFAR-100 to validate primary hypothesis: reduced feature suppression through adaptive pair weighting

steps.step1: Implement Feature Diversity Estimation Module (FDEM): (a) Compute activation frequency f_d = mean(1[z_{j,d} > η]), (b) Compute rarity score r_{ip} = sum_d 1[(z_i,d>η)∧(z_p,d>η)] / (f_d + ε), (c) Compute weights w_{ip} = softmax(r_{ip}/τ_w) * |P(i)|
steps.step2: Implement FD-SCL loss using weighted positive pairs: L = -1/|P| * sum_{p in P} w_{ip} * log(exp(z_i·z_p/τ) / sum_{a in A} exp(z_i·z_a/τ))
steps.step3: Hyperparameters: τ=0.1 (contrastive temp), τ_w=0.5 (weight temp), η=0.0 (activation threshold), ε=1e-6
steps.step4: Architecture: Same as SCL (ResNet-18 + MLP projector) for fair comparison
steps.step5: Training: Same schedule as SCL (200 epochs encoder + 100 epochs linear eval)
steps.step8: Save weight statistics for analysis: results/fdscl_weights_seed{N}.npz
steps.step9: Record training time and verify <10% overhead vs SCL

title: CIFAR-100 Coarse-to-Fine Feature Suppression Evaluation
description: PRIMARY EVALUATION: Train with coarse (20 superclasses) labels, evaluate on fine (100 classes) labels to measure feature suppression. This directly tests if FD-SCL preserves subclass features.

steps.step1: Create coarse-label training setup: Map 100 fine CIFAR-100 classes to 20 coarse superclasses using official mapping: {'aquatic_mammals': [4, 30, 55, 72, 95], 'fish': [1, 32, 67, 73, 91], ...}
steps.step2: Train SCL with coarse labels only (20 classes) for 150 epochs (reduced for time budget)
steps.step3: Train FD-SCL with coarse labels only (20 classes) for 150 epochs (reduced for time budget)
steps.step4: After training, evaluate linear classifier on: (a) Coarse labels (20 classes), (b) Fine labels (100 classes) - KEY METRIC
steps.step5: Compute: Fine-grained accuracy gap = FD-SCL_fine_acc - SCL_fine_acc. SUCCESS CRITERIA: gap >= 5%
steps.step8: This is the most important experiment - prioritize running this first

title: Ablation 1: Weight Temperature τ_w Sensitivity
description: Test sensitivity to weight temperature hyperparameter. Success: FD-SCL robust to τ_w in reasonable range.

steps.step1: Train FD-SCL on CIFAR-100 with τ_w values: [0.3, 0.5, 0.7] (reduced set for time)
steps.step2: Use fixed seed=42 for all runs to isolate hyperparameter effect
steps.step3: Use 50 epochs only to save time (sufficient for relative comparison)
steps.step4: Measure: Final test accuracy, effective rank of representations
steps.step5: Expected: Performance stable for τ_w in [0.3, 0.7], optimal around 0.5

title: Ablation 2: Activation Threshold η Robustness
description: Test robustness to activation threshold choice. Verify method works across reasonable threshold values.

steps.step1: Train FD-SCL on CIFAR-100 with η values: [-0.5, 0.0, 0.5] (0.0 is default)
steps.step3: Measure: Test accuracy, training time overhead
steps.step4: Expected: Minimal sensitivity to η around 0.0

title: Ablation 3: Component Analysis
description: Verify each component of FDEM is necessary. Remove components one at a time.

steps.step1: Variant 1: Uniform weights (all w_ip = 1) - equivalent to standard SCL
steps.step2: Variant 2: Random weights (w_ip ~ Uniform[0.5, 1.5]) - sanity check
steps.step3: Variant 3: Full FD-SCL with rarity-based weighting
steps.step4: Train all variants on CIFAR-100 with seed=42, 100 epochs
steps.step5: Measure: Test accuracy, convergence speed, feature diversity (effective rank)
steps.step6: Success: Full FD-SCL > Uniform weights > Random weights

title: Feature Diversity Metrics Computation
description: Compute effective rank and other diversity metrics to quantify feature suppression reduction (Success Criteria 3)

steps.step1: For each trained model (SCL and FD-SCL, all seeds), extract embeddings for test set
steps.step2: Compute class-wise covariance matrices: For each class c, compute Σ_c = Cov(embeddings[y==c])
steps.step3: Compute effective rank: erank(Σ) = exp(H(p)) where p_i = λ_i / sum(λ), H is Shannon entropy, λ_i are eigenvalues of Σ
steps.step4: Compute average effective rank across all classes
steps.step5: SUCCESS CRITERIA: FD-SCL effective rank >= 1.15 × SCL effective rank (15% improvement)
steps.step6: Also compute: singular value spectra, participation ratio

title: k-NN Evaluation of Learned Representations
description: Evaluate representation quality without linear classifier using k-NN accuracy. Tests if features are inherently more separable.

steps.step1: Extract frozen embeddings from trained encoders (SCL, FD-SCL) on test set
steps.step2: Compute k-NN accuracy with k=200 (standard for contrastive learning)
steps.step3: Use L2 normalized embeddings, cosine similarity
steps.step5: Success: FD-SCL k-NN accuracy >= SCL k-NN accuracy + 1%

title: Statistical Significance Testing
description: Perform statistical tests to ensure improvements are significant, not due to random variation

steps.step1: Collect results from 3 random seeds for each method on each dataset
steps.step2: Compute mean and standard deviation for all metrics
steps.step3: Perform paired t-test between SCL and FD-SCL results
steps.step5: Success: p < 0.05 for primary metrics (coarse-to-fine accuracy)

title: t-SNE Visualization of Embeddings
description: Create t-SNE visualizations showing better subclass separation with FD-SCL vs SCL

steps.step1: Extract embeddings for CIFAR-100 test set from SCL and FD-SCL (seed=42)
steps.step3: Create plots: (a) Colored by coarse labels (20 classes), (b) Colored by fine labels (sample 20 for clarity)
steps.step4: Visual inspection: FD-SCL should show tighter clusters for subclasses within each superclass

title: Weight Distribution Analysis
description: Visualize the distribution of pair weights to verify FDEM upweights rare-feature pairs

steps.step1: Load saved weight statistics from FD-SCL training
steps.step2: Analyze correlation between weight and feature rarity: compute Pearson correlation between w_{ip} and r_{ip}
steps.step4: Plot weight vs rarity score scatter plot
steps.step5: Expected: Strong positive correlation, high variance in weights (not uniform)

title: Training Curves and Convergence Analysis
description: Plot training loss curves, test accuracy over epochs, and convergence speed comparison

steps.step1: Load training logs for all methods and seeds
steps.step2: Plot: (a) Training loss vs epoch, (b) Test accuracy vs epoch, (c) Linear eval accuracy vs epoch
steps.step3: Include mean and shaded std across 3 seeds
steps.step4: Plot effective rank evolution during training

title: Results Summary Tables and Figures
description: Create publication-ready tables and figures for paper

steps.step1: Table 1: Main results on CIFAR-100 (standard accuracy, coarse-to-fine accuracy, k-NN, effective rank)
steps.step2: Table 2: Ablation study results (τ_w, η, components)
steps.step3: Figure 1: t-SNE visualizations comparing SCL vs FD-SCL
steps.step4: Figure 2: Training curves and convergence comparison
steps.step5: Figure 3: Weight distribution and rarity correlation
steps.step6: Figure 4: Effective rank comparison across methods
steps.step7: Format tables: Mean ± std over 3 seeds, bold best results
steps.step8: Export as LaTeX tables and high-res PDF figures: logs/tables/, logs/figures/

title: Final Success Criteria Validation
description: Evaluate all experiments against the success criteria defined in the proposal and generate final report

steps.step1: Criterion 1: Feature Suppression Reduction - Check if FD-SCL subclass accuracy >= SCL + 5% on CIFAR-100 coarse-to-fine
steps.step2: Criterion 2: Standard Classification - Check if FD-SCL maintains or improves standard CIFAR-100 accuracy
steps.step3: Criterion 3: Representation Quality - Check if FD-SCL effective rank >= 1.15 × SCL effective rank
steps.step4: Criterion 4: Computational Efficiency - Check if FD-SCL overhead < 10% vs SCL
steps.step5: Generate summary report: results/success_criteria_report.md with PASS/FAIL for each criterion
steps.step6: Document any failure modes and potential mitigations
steps.step7: Create executive summary: results/executive_summary.txt with key findings
